message:
perf(hpack): replace linear scan with grouped code-length Huffman decoder

Rewrite the HPACK Huffman decoder to match codes by bit-length groups
(5, 6, 7, 8, ...) instead of scanning all 257 entries per symbol.
The shortest Huffman codes are 5 bits, so the decoder consumes at
least 5 bits per iteration, bounding work per input byte to a small
constant regardless of input content.

The previous approach had a fast path for 5-bit codes but fell back
to a linear scan of the full table for longer codes, making it
vulnerable to CPU exhaustion on crafted inputs using only long codes.

Also adds explicit 6-bit and 7-bit fast paths with match tables for
the most common HTTP header characters, reducing branch mispredictions
for typical header traffic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
